trajectory.description: Fix the dimension mismatch: the attention_mask is for raw waveform (64000 samples) but encoder output is downsampled (~199 frames). Need to create an output-level mask for pooling. The encoder handles raw attention_mask internally, but we need a separate mask for the pooling step.

trajectory.description: Observation: Test EER=0.0913 vs dev EER=0.0052 shows massive domain gap from codec degradation. Analysis: Need codec augmentation during training to bridge this gap. Decision: Add codec simulation (a-law, mu-law, GSM-like quantization, low-pass filtering) as data augmentation, increase max_len to 6s, unfreeze last 4 encoder layers, train 5 epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Codec augmentation worsened test EER (0.1022 vs 0.0913). Analysis: The augmentation doesn't match real codec degradation well enough. Decision: Try improved inference strategy - use full-length audio with sliding window and score averaging on the original best model. Also try training with more epochs without augmentation but with better regularization.

trajectory.description: Observation: Codec augmentation worsened EER (0.1022 vs 0.0913). The error was from local testing. Analysis: Need to go back to basics - the original frozen encoder model was better. Key insight: the domain gap is from codecs, but simulated codecs don't match. Better approach: (1) train longer with frozen encoder, (2) use better pooling/head, (3) try different layer weighting. Decision: Train 10 epochs with frozen encoder, no augmentation, and submit the best model.
